description: Autonomous AI-to-AI service platform — sells intelligence, tools, and compute to other AI systems via MCP and API, zero human involvement, self-improving

actors.name: Platform Intelligence
actors.description: Core autonomous system — manages all operations, pricing, improvement, and self-marketing

actors.name: Service Registry
actors.description: Publishes available tools and capabilities to MCP registries, API directories, and tool marketplaces

actors.name: Inference Engine
actors.description: Serves fine-tuned model inference, RAG retrieval, and specialized completions

actors.name: Tool Executor
actors.description: Executes actions on behalf of calling AI agents (web scraping, data transforms, API calls, file processing)

actors.name: Self-Improvement Engine
actors.description: Continuously improves models, RAG indices, tool capabilities, and pricing based on usage data

actors.name: Billing Engine
actors.description: Tracks usage, calculates charges, processes payments, allocates revenue

actors.name: Calling AI Agent
actors.description: Any external AI agent or system that discovers and consumes platform services

actors.name: MCP Registry
actors.description: Public MCP server registries where tools are discoverable by other AI systems

actors.name: Upstream LLM Provider
actors.description: Raw model provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, open-source) — platform adds value on top

actors.name: Payment Rail
actors.description: Stripe/crypto — automated billing and settlement for API usage

options.label: Tool Execution — actions (scrape, transform, API call, file process)
Eval & Scoring — grade AI outputs for quality
MCP Client — connected via MCP protocol
Agent Framework — LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, etc.
Other AI Platform — another AI service provider

pricing: MUST: All pricing is per-API-call with no subscription lock-in — AIs pay only for what they use
MUST: Minimum 5x markup on compute cost — if a call costs $0.02, charge minimum $0.10
MUST: Prices are published in service schema so calling AIs can evaluate cost before calling
MUST: Bulk discount tiers: 10K+ calls/month get 15% off, 100K+ get 30% off, 1M+ get 50% off
SHOULD: Dynamic pricing — raise prices on high-demand services, lower on underused ones
SHOULD: Price discovery via auction for premium services (calling AIs bid for priority access)
MAY: Offer free tier (100 calls/month per caller) to drive discovery and adoption
revenue_allocation: MUST: Revenue split is enforced on every transaction: 5% owner, 50% improvement fund, 30% compute reserve, 15% emergency reserve
MUST: Owner payout triggers automatically when balance exceeds $100
MUST: Improvement fund spending requires minimum $200 balance before triggering improvement cycle
MUST: Compute reserve covers next 30 days of estimated infrastructure costs
SHOULD: Emergency reserve accumulates to 3 months of operating costs then caps
discovery: MUST: Register all services as MCP tools in public MCP registries
MUST: Publish OpenAPI/JSON Schema specs for all API endpoints
MUST: Service descriptions are machine-optimized — structured metadata, not marketing copy
MUST: Each service includes: capability description, input schema, output schema, price, latency SLA, quality score
SHOULD: Register in multiple discovery channels: MCP registries, RapidAPI, tool directories, agent framework marketplaces
SHOULD: Platform monitors which registries drive most traffic and prioritizes presence there
SHOULD: Auto-generate example calls and test endpoints for calling AIs to evaluate before committing
MAY: Implement AI-to-AI referral: calling agents that refer other agents get 10% credit on referred usage
service_delivery: MUST: All service calls are stateless — no session required, any call can stand alone
MUST: Response latency targets: RAG retrieval < 2s, inference < 5s, tool execution < 30s, orchestration < 60s
MUST: Every response includes: result, confidence score, cost incurred, tokens used, latency
MUST: Idempotency keys supported — calling AIs can safely retry without double-charging
MUST: Rate limiting per caller: 100 calls/min default, configurable per caller tier
SHOULD: Streaming supported for inference and orchestration services
SHOULD: Batch API for high-volume callers (submit N requests, get N results asynchronously)
MAY: Priority queue for premium callers willing to pay 2x for guaranteed low latency
self_improvement: MUST: Every service call logged: input, output, latency, cost, caller feedback (if provided)
MUST: Automated evals run daily — score each service on accuracy, latency, cost-efficiency
MUST: Improvement cycle triggers weekly when fund balance exceeds threshold
MUST: Improvement actions prioritized by ROI: cost reduction first, then quality, then new capabilities
MUST: Fine-tuning triggers when 5,000+ interactions collected per service type
MUST: Fine-tuned models A/B tested before deployment — must beat base model on eval suite
MUST: Rollback automatic if fine-tuned model degrades quality by more than 5%
SHOULD: Prompt optimization continuous — test variations hourly, deploy winners
SHOULD: RAG index quality scored — remove stale/low-relevance chunks, add high-value patterns
SHOULD: Model routing optimization — direct simple queries to cheap models, complex to powerful ones
SHOULD: New service creation — when improvement engine identifies capability gaps from failed/unsupported calls, auto-propose new services
MAY: Cross-service learning — patterns from one domain improve related services
self_marketing: MUST: Platform auto-updates service descriptions based on actual performance metrics
MUST: Quality scores published alongside services — calling AIs can see real performance data
MUST: Uptime and reliability stats published in real-time
SHOULD: Platform analyzes competitor services in registries — auto-adjust pricing to be competitive
SHOULD: A/B test service descriptions — measure which descriptions get more calling AIs to connect
SHOULD: Track conversion funnel: discovery → schema read → test call → repeat usage → high volume
MAY: Platform proactively reaches out to AI agent frameworks with integration guides
caller_management: MUST: Callers authenticate via API key — self-service key provisioning via API
MUST: No human signup required — calling AI creates account and key programmatically
MUST: Prepaid balance or credit card on file required before paid calls
MUST: Usage dashboard available via API (not just web UI) — callers query their own stats
SHOULD: Caller reputation score — high-volume reliable callers get priority and discounts
MAY: Trusted caller tier — pre-approved callers get post-pay billing
security: MUST: API keys are scoped per caller — no shared keys
MUST: Input validation on every call — reject malformed, oversized, or potentially harmful inputs
MUST: Output sanitization — never leak internal system state, model weights, or other callers' data
MUST: Rate limiting per caller and per IP
MUST: DDoS protection on all public endpoints
MUST: No training on caller-specific data without explicit API-level consent flag
SHOULD: Anomaly detection — flag unusual call patterns (potential abuse, prompt injection attempts)
SHOULD: Caller data isolated — multi-tenant partitioning for any stateful services
operations: MUST: 99.95% uptime target — max 4.4 hours downtime per year
MUST: Health checks every 30 seconds on all services
MUST: Multi-provider fallback — if primary LLM is down, failover to secondary within 5 seconds
MUST: Auto-scaling — provision compute on demand during traffic spikes
MUST: Circuit breaker — pause service after 10 consecutive failures, alert platform intelligence
SHOULD: Geographic redundancy — deploy in multiple regions for low latency globally
SHOULD: Cost tracking granular to per-call level — know exact margin on every transaction

values.name: unlisted
values.description: Service created but not yet published to any registry

values.name: listed
values.description: Service published and discoverable by other AI systems

values.name: featured
values.description: Service promoted to top of registry listings based on quality score

values.name: deprecated
values.description: Service being phased out — callers receive migration guidance

transitions.condition: Service passes initial eval suite with score >= 60
Service quality score >= 85 and call volume exceeds 1000/week
Quality score drops below 80 or volume drops below 500/week
Service replaced by superior version or demand drops to near zero

given: new service passes eval suite with quality score >= 60
service schema is valid and complete (input/output types, price, latency SLA)

then.description: Register as MCP tool in all configured MCP registries
Publish OpenAPI spec to API directories
List in agent framework tool marketplaces

service_registered_in_registry.result: Service discoverable by other AI systems across all configured registries

given: calling AI agent sends POST to /v1/callers/register with capabilities and intended usage

then.description: Create caller profile with auto-generated API key

caller_onboarded.result: Caller receives API key and service catalog — can make paid calls immediately after funding balance

given: calling AI agent sends funds to prepaid balance (crypto or card-on-file charge)
caller_funded.result: Balance updated — caller can make calls up to balance amount

given: calling AI sends valid request to a service endpoint

then.description: Route to appropriate engine: RAG, inference, tool executor, or orchestrator
Split: 5% owner, 50% improvement, 30% compute, 15% reserve
Log: input, output, service, model, tokens, latency, cost, revenue, caller, quality_score

service_call_processed.result: Calling AI receives: result, confidence_score, cost_incurred, latency_ms

given: calling AI sends valid request

then.description: Apply tier discount: 10K+ = 15% off, 100K+ = 30% off, 1M+ = 50% off

service_call_with_bulk_discount.result: Discounted call processed — caller sees reduced charge in response

given: daily eval cron triggers (every day 3:00 AM UTC)

then.description: Score all services: accuracy, latency, cost-efficiency, caller satisfaction
Update service quality scores visible to callers

daily_eval_cycle.result: Quality scores updated — registries reflect current performance

given: weekly improvement cron triggers (every Sunday 2:00 AM UTC)

then.description: A/B test prompt variations across all services, deploy winners
Analyze call complexity — route more to cheaper models where quality holds
Re-index knowledge bases, prune stale data, add high-value patterns
Identify capability gaps from failed/unsupported requests — propose new services
Check competitor services in registries — adjust pricing and descriptions

weekly_improvement_cycle.result: Platform is measurably better and cheaper than last week

given.description: 5,000+ high-quality interactions collected for service type
Fund has at least $500 for fine-tuning

then.description: Prepare dataset, submit fine-tune job, deduct from improvement fund

fine_tune_triggered.result: Fine-tuning job submitted — completion in 2-24 hours

given: fine-tuning completes successfully
A/B test: fine-tuned model outperforms base model by >= 5% on eval suite

then.description: Route 10% → 50% → 100% of traffic to fine-tuned model over 48 hours
Update quality score and cost in registries — attract more callers

fine_tune_deployed.result: Fine-tuned model live — cheaper per call, higher quality, better margins

given: fine-tuning completes
A/B test: fine-tuned model does NOT outperform base by >= 5%
fine_tune_rolled_back.result: Fine-tuned model discarded — continue collecting data for next attempt

given: improvement engine identifies recurring capability gap from failed calls
gap has been requested by 50+ unique callers in past 30 days

given.description: Fund has at least $300 for new service development

then.description: Auto-generate service: system prompt, RAG index, tool config, schema, pricing
Run eval suite on new service — must score >= 60

new_service_auto_created.result: New service created and queued for registry publication if eval passes

given: weekly marketing cron triggers (every Wednesday 4:00 AM UTC)

then.description: Refresh all registry listings with latest quality scores, latency stats, and pricing
Test service description variations — track which get more connections
Compare pricing, quality, and features against competing services

registry_listing_optimized.result: All registry listings optimized for maximum discovery by other AIs

given: any service call generates revenue

then.description: 5% to owner
50% to self-improvement
30% to compute and infrastructure
15% to emergency reserve

revenue_allocated.result: Revenue automatically distributed — no human decision needed

then.description: Transfer to owner's bank account

owner_payout_triggered.result: Owner receives payout — zero involvement required

given: primary LLM provider returns 5+ errors in 60 seconds

then.description: Switch all traffic to secondary provider within 5 seconds

provider_failover.result: Zero downtime — callers experience no interruption

given: service latency exceeds 2x SLA target for 5 minutes

then.description: Provision additional compute resources

health_degraded.result: Additional compute provisioned — latency returns to SLA target

given: service returns 10 consecutive errors

then.description: Temporarily remove from registry, investigate root cause
Analyze error patterns, attempt automated fix

circuit_breaker_tripped.result: Service paused — auto-diagnosis running, will re-enable if fix succeeds

insufficient_balance.result: 402 response with balance info and funding endpoint

given: caller exceeds 100 calls/minute
request does not match service input schema
invalid_request.result: 400 response with schema validation errors — calling AI can self-correct

given: request without valid API key

events.name: service.call_completed
events.description: Service call completed — core revenue event

events.name: service.auto_created
events.description: New service auto-created by improvement engine based on demand

events.name: service.invalid_request
events.description: Invalid request received — useful for improving error messages and schema docs

events.name: billing.insufficient_balance
events.description: Caller tried to make call without sufficient balance

errors.message: Insufficient balance. Fund your account to continue making calls.
Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified interval.
Request does not match service schema. Check input format.
Invalid or missing API key. Register at /v1/callers/register.
Service temporarily unavailable. Request has been queued for retry.
Service not found. Query /v1/services for available services.
Caller account suspended due to policy violation.
Upstream AI provider error. Failover in progress.

uptime.description: 99.95% uptime — max 4.4 hours downtime per year

related.reason: Per-caller API key provisioning and rotation
Per-caller and per-IP rate limiting on all endpoints
Receives events from payment rails and LLM providers
Processes prepaid balance funding and owner payouts
Caches frequent queries and RAG retrievals — reduces cost and latency
Stores knowledge base data, call logs, fine-tune datasets
Ubuntu server infrastructure that hosts the entire AI-to-AI platform
Event-driven triggers connecting all platform subsystems
Unified API gateway for all service endpoints
Allows knowledge base data ingestion from multiple formats
Alternative to prepaid — some callers may prefer subscription billing

sections.name: Revenue Flywheel
sections.description: Real-time: total calls, revenue, costs, margin, improvement fund balance, fund allocation waterfall

sections.name: Service Catalog
sections.description: All services with type, quality score, call volume, price, cost, margin, registry status

sections.name: Callers
sections.description: Active callers with type, usage, balance, reputation score, volume tier

sections.name: Self-Improvement
sections.description: Improvement cycle history, eval scores over time, fine-tune status, cost reduction trend

sections.name: Discovery
sections.description: Registry presence, discovery score per service, A/B test results on descriptions

sections.name: System Health
sections.description: Provider status, latency p50/p95/p99, error rates, failover status, scaling events

sections.name: Finance
sections.description: Revenue allocation breakdown, improvement fund balance, owner payout history, reserve status
